app.name: LLMCore
app.description: Self-hosted LLM provider abstraction layer with agentic capabilities, RAG, embeddings, observability, and sandbox execution

output.header_comment:
LLMCore configuration — generated by confy-curator.
User config location: ~/.config/llmcore/config.toml
Environment variable prefix: LLMCORE_
Nested keys use double underscores: LLMCORE_PROVIDERS__OPENAI__API_KEY

Re-run:  confy-curator update -s llmcore.confy-schema.json -c config.toml

cuda_version.command: nvidia-smi 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'CUDA Version: \K[0-9.]+' || nvcc --version 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'release \K[0-9.]+' || (test -f /usr/local/cuda/version.txt && head -1 /usr/local/cuda/version.txt | grep -oP '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+') || echo ''

total_ram_gb.command: free -g 2>/dev/null | awk '/^Mem:/{print $2}' || sysctl -n hw.memsize 2>/dev/null | awk '{print int($1/1073741824)}' || echo 8
total_ram_gb.description: Detect total system RAM in GB

os_type.description: Detect OS type (Linux, Darwin, FreeBSD, etc.)

docker_available.command: docker info >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo true || echo false
docker_available.description: Check if Docker daemon is available

ollama_available.command: curl -sf http://localhost:11434/api/version >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo true || echo false
ollama_available.description: Check if Ollama server is running locally

pg_available.command: (pg_isready -q -h localhost 2>/dev/null || pg_isready -q -h localhost -p 5433 2>/dev/null || pg_isready -q -h localhost -p 5434 2>/dev/null) && echo true || echo false
pg_available.description: Check if PostgreSQL is reachable via TCP on localhost (ports 5432-5434)

pg_port.command: (pg_isready -q -h localhost -p 5432 2>/dev/null && echo 5432) || (pg_isready -q -h localhost -p 5433 2>/dev/null && echo 5433) || (pg_isready -q -h localhost -p 5434 2>/dev/null && echo 5434) || echo 0
pg_port.description: Detect which port PostgreSQL is listening on (TCP, localhost)

hf_token_available.description: Check if a Hugging Face API token is set

sections.title: Core Settings
sections.description: Fundamental llmcore configuration — provider selection, embedding model, and diagnostics.

fields.title: Default Provider
fields.description: Specifies which provider instance (from [providers] below) to use by default when no provider is explicitly chosen. Allows you to switch between different LLM backends quickly. "openai", "anthropic", "gemini", "openrouter").

options.label: Hugging Face
options.description: Open-weight models via HF Inference API

options.label: Poe
options.description: Gateway to models and community bots

fields.title: Default Embedding Model
fields.description: Specifies which embedding model to use for RAG operations. Required for generating vector embeddings of text chunks in RAG. - For local models (Sentence Transformers): A model name from the Hugging Face model hub (e.g., "all-MiniLM-L6-v2", "all-mpnet-base-v2"). The model will be downloaded automatically. - For service-based models: A string in the format "provider:model_name" (e.g., "openai:text-embedding-3-small", "google:gemini-embedding-001").
fields.recommendation: For development, 'all-MiniLM-L6-v2' (local, zero-cost). For production with OpenAI, 'openai:text-embedding-3-small'.

fields.title: Log Level
fields.description: Configures the logging verbosity for the entire `llmcore` library. Control the amount of diagnostic information emitted by the library.

fields.title: Log Raw Payloads
fields.description: If true, `llmcore` will log the exact raw JSON request and response payloads exchanged with the underlying LLM provider APIs. This logging occurs at the `DEBUG` log level. An indispensable tool for advanced troubleshooting. It allows you to inspect the precise data being sent and received, which is essential for diagnosing provider errors or understanding unexpected behavior.

warning_if.message: Raw payload logging may include sensitive data (API keys, user content). Use only for debugging.

fields.title: Admin Api Key
fields.description: Defines the secret key required for accessing administrative endpoints such as live configuration reloading. This provides a separate, high-privilege authentication layer independent of tenant API keys. Secures administrative operations that could impact the entire platform, ensuring only authorized administrators can perform sensitive actions like configuration reloads. This key should be treated as a high-privilege secret. In production, ALWAYS set this via the environment variable rather than in config files.
fields.recommendation: Always set via the LLMCORE_ADMIN_API_KEY environment variable in production, not in config files.

sections.title: Unified Logging
sections.description: Logging for all components: llmcore, llmchat, semantiscan, and confy. Single source of truth.

fields.title: Console Enabled
fields.description: Console logging - set to false to suppress most console output. When false, only messages logged with display=True (via log_display()) will appear on the console.  Set to true for verbose/debug sessions.

fields.title: File Enabled
fields.description: File logging - always recommended to keep enabled

fields.title: File Mode
fields.description: File mode: "per_run" (new file each invocation) or "single" (persistent + rotation)

fields.title: File Single Name
fields.description: Single-file mode settings (ignored when file_mode = "per_run"):

fields.title: Display Min Level
fields.description: Minimum level for display=True records on console (even when console_enabled=false)

fields.title: Urllib3
fields.description: Suppress noisy third-party libraries

sections.title: Provider: OpenAI
sections.description: GPT-4o, GPT-5, o3, o4-mini, and any OpenAI-compatible API.

fields.title: Base Url
fields.description: Override for proxies or OpenAI-compatible APIs (Groq, Together, etc.). Leave empty for official OpenAI.

fields.title: Api Key
fields.description: OpenAI API key. Strongly recommended to set via OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable.
fields.recommendation: Set via OPENAI_API_KEY or LLMCORE_PROVIDERS__OPENAI__API_KEY environment variable.

fields.title: Default Model
fields.description: Default model for this provider instance.

discover.command:
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json, os, sys
P = 'openai'
dirs = [os.path.join('src', 'llmcore')]
dirs += [os.path.join(p, 'llmcore') for p in sys.path if os.path.isdir(os.path.join(p, 'llmcore', 'model_cards'))]
for b in [os.path.join(d, 'model_cards', 'default_cards', P) for d in dirs]:
    if not os.path.isdir(b): continue
    r = []
    for f in sorted(os.listdir(b)):
        if not f.endswith('.json'): continue
        try:
            d = json.load(open(os.path.join(b, f)))
            if d.get('model_type') != 'chat': continue
            if d.get('lifecycle', {}).get('status') in ('retired', 'deprecated'): continue
            ctx = d.get('context', {}).get('max_input_tokens', '?')
            pi = d.get('pricing', {}).get('per_million_tokens', {}).get('input', '?')
            r.append({'value': d['model_id'], 'label': d.get('display_name', d['model_id']), 'description': f'{ctx} ctx, ${pi}/M in'})
        except Exception: pass
    print(json.dumps(r))
    sys.exit()
print('[]')
PYEOF
discover.description: Load available openai chat models from llmcore model cards

fields.title: Timeout
fields.description: Request timeout in seconds.

sections.title: Provider: Anthropic
sections.description: Claude 4.x family.

fields.title: Default Max Tokens
fields.description: Default max_tokens for Anthropic responses (required parameter). Override per-request.

fields.title: Api Key
fields.description: Anthropic API key. Strongly recommended to set via ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable.
fields.recommendation: Set via ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or LLMCORE_PROVIDERS__ANTHROPIC__API_KEY environment variable.

fields.title: Default Model
fields.description: Default model for this provider instance. Use the dash format (canonical API model IDs), not dot format. Current models (April 2026): claude-opus-4-6, claude-sonnet-4-6          (Claude 4.6 family) claude-opus-4-5, claude-sonnet-4-5           (Claude 4.5 family) claude-haiku-4-5, claude-haiku-4-5-20251001  (Claude 4.5 Haiku) claude-opus-4-1, claude-opus-4-0, claude-sonnet-4-0 Dated variants (e.g., claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929) are also accepted.

discover.command:
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json, os, sys
P = 'anthropic'
dirs = [os.path.join('src', 'llmcore')]
dirs += [os.path.join(p, 'llmcore') for p in sys.path if os.path.isdir(os.path.join(p, 'llmcore', 'model_cards'))]
for b in [os.path.join(d, 'model_cards', 'default_cards', P) for d in dirs]:
    if not os.path.isdir(b): continue
    r = []
    for f in sorted(os.listdir(b)):
        if not f.endswith('.json'): continue
        try:
            d = json.load(open(os.path.join(b, f)))
            if d.get('model_type') != 'chat': continue
            if d.get('lifecycle', {}).get('status') in ('retired', 'deprecated'): continue
            ctx = d.get('context', {}).get('max_input_tokens', '?')
            pi = d.get('pricing', {}).get('per_million_tokens', {}).get('input', '?')
            r.append({'value': d['model_id'], 'label': d.get('display_name', d['model_id']), 'description': f'{ctx} ctx, ${pi}/M in'})
        except Exception: pass
    print(json.dumps(r))
    sys.exit()
print('[]')
PYEOF
discover.description: Load available anthropic chat models from llmcore model cards

fields.title: Timeout
fields.description: Request timeout in seconds. Extended thinking responses can take 30-90s; 120s accommodates this.

sections.title: Provider: Ollama
sections.description: Locally running models via Ollama.

fields.title: Host
fields.description: URL of the running Ollama server.

fields.title: Default Model
fields.description: Default local model to use. Ensure you have run `ollama pull <model_name>`.

discover.description: Fetch locally pulled Ollama models from running server

transform.jmespath: models[].{value: name, label: name, description: details.family}

fields.title: Timeout
fields.description: Longer timeout is recommended for local models.

sections.title: Provider: Google Gemini
sections.description: Gemini models via Google AI or Vertex AI.

fields.title: GCP Location
fields.description: GCP region for Vertex AI.

fields.title: GCP Project
fields.description: GCP project ID for Vertex AI.

fields.title: Use Vertex AI
fields.description: Use Vertex AI backend instead of the Gemini API. Requires GCP authentication.

fields.title: Api Key
fields.description: Google AI API key. Set via GOOGLE_API_KEY environment variable.
fields.recommendation: Set via GOOGLE_API_KEY or LLMCORE_PROVIDERS__GEMINI__API_KEY environment variable.

fields.title: Default Model
fields.description: Default model for this provider instance. Current models (April 2026): 3.x (preview):  gemini-3.1-pro-preview, gemini-3-flash-preview, gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview 2.5 (stable):   gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite 2.0 (deprecated, shutdown June 2026): gemini-2.0-flash, gemini-2.0-flash-lite

fields.title: Fallback Context Length
fields.description: Fallback context length when model is not in the card registry or API. Most current Gemini models support 1M tokens.

sections.title: Provider: OpenRouter
sections.description: Unified gateway to 300+ AI models.

fields.title: Api Key Env Var
fields.description: type = "openrouter"  # auto-detected from section name API Key: Set via environment variable for security. api_key = "sk-or-..."

fields.title: Default Model
fields.description: Default model.  OpenRouter uses "provider/model" format. "google/gemini-3-flash-preview", "deepseek/deepseek-chat"

discover.description: Load available openrouter chat models from llmcore model cards

fields.title: Backend
fields.description: "openai"  (default): Uses the `openai` Python SDK with base_url override. No extra dependencies beyond `openai`. "sdk"   : Uses the native `openrouter` Python SDK (pip install openrouter). Falls back to "openai" mode if the package is not installed.

sections.title: Provider: Poe
sections.description: Gateway to models and community bots.

fields.title: Api Key Env Var
fields.description: type = "poe"  # auto-detected from section name API Key: Set via environment variable for security. api_key = "..."

fields.title: Default Model
fields.description: Default bot to use.  Bot names are case-sensitive as registered on Poe. "Gemini-3.1-Pro", "Grok-4", "DeepSeek-V3.2-Exp", "Sora-2", "Veo-3.1" (video), "GPT-Image-1.5" (images)

discover.description: Load available poe chat models from llmcore model cards

fields.title: Timeout
fields.description: Request timeout in seconds.  Video/image generation bots may need higher values (300+) since they perform async long-running work.

fields.title: Backend
fields.description: "openai" (default): Uses the `openai` Python SDK with base_url override. All standard OpenAI-compatible features supported. No extra dependencies beyond `openai`. "native" : Uses the native `fastapi_poe` SSE client (pip install fastapi-poe). Required for: - Poe-specific custom bot parameters not in OpenAI schema - File attachments via the Attachment protocol - Bot-to-bot invocation (when running as a Poe server bot) Falls back to "openai" mode if the package is not installed or any request error occurs.

sections.title: Provider: vLLM
sections.description: Self-hosted vLLM inference server.

fields.title: Base Url
fields.description: Base URL of your vLLM server (including /v1). Required — no default because vLLM is self-hosted.

fields.title: Default Model
fields.description: Default model name — HuggingFace repo id or whatever name you passed to `vllm serve ...`.  Override per-request via chat_completion(model=...).

discover.description: Query vLLM server for currently served models

fields.title: Timeout
fields.description: Request timeout in seconds.  Local cold-starts and large models can be slow; 240s covers most practical cases.

sections.title: Provider: Mistral AI
sections.description: Native Mistral AI platform provider.

fields.title: Base Url
fields.description: Override for custom Mistral endpoints. Leave empty for official API.

fields.title: Api Key Env Var
fields.description: type = "mistral"  # auto-detected from section name API Key: Set via environment variable for security. api_key = "..."

fields.title: Default Model
fields.description: Default model. Current models (April 2026): Frontier:   mistral-large-latest (→ mistral-large-2512) Medium:     mistral-medium-latest (→ mistral-medium-2508) Small:      mistral-small-latest (→ mistral-small-2506) Reasoning:  magistral-medium-latest, magistral-small-latest Code:       codestral-latest, devstral-2-latest Ministral:  ministral-3-14b-latest, ministral-3-8b-latest, ministral-3-3b-latest Audio STT:  voxtral-mini-latest (transcription) OCR:        mistral-ocr-latest Embedding:  mistral-embed, codestral-embed-2505

discover.command:
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json, os, sys
P = 'mistral'
dirs = [os.path.join('src', 'llmcore')]
dirs += [os.path.join(p, 'llmcore') for p in sys.path if os.path.isdir(os.path.join(p, 'llmcore', 'model_cards'))]
for b in [os.path.join(d, 'model_cards', 'default_cards', P) for d in dirs]:
    if not os.path.isdir(b): continue
    r = []
    for f in sorted(os.listdir(b)):
        if not f.endswith('.json'): continue
        try:
            d = json.load(open(os.path.join(b, f)))
            if d.get('model_type') != 'chat': continue
            if d.get('lifecycle', {}).get('status') in ('retired', 'deprecated'): continue
            ctx = d.get('context', {}).get('max_input_tokens', '?')
            pi = d.get('pricing', {}).get('per_million_tokens', {}).get('input', '?')
            r.append({'value': d['model_id'], 'label': d.get('display_name', d['model_id']), 'description': f'{ctx} ctx, ${pi}/M in'})
        except Exception: pass
    print(json.dumps(r))
    sys.exit()
print('[]')

discover.description: Load available mistral chat models from llmcore model cards

fields.title: Timeout
fields.description: Request timeout in seconds.

sections.title: Provider: Hugging Face
sections.description: Open-weight models via the Hugging Face Inference API.  Supports chat, vision, embeddings, TTS, ASR, and image generation.  Requests can be routed through third-party providers (Together, SambaNova, Cerebras, etc.).

fields.title: API Key
fields.description: HuggingFace API token (starts with hf_...).  Strongly recommended to set via HF_TOKEN environment variable.
fields.recommendation: Set via HF_TOKEN or LLMCORE_PROVIDERS__HUGGINGFACE__API_KEY environment variable.

fields.title: API Key Env Var
fields.description: Environment variable name to read the API key from.

fields.title: Default Model
fields.description: Default chat/instruct model.  Any model hosted on the Hugging Face Hub that is served by the Inference API can be used.

discover.command:
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json, os, sys
P = 'huggingface'
dirs = [os.path.join('src', 'llmcore')]
dirs += [os.path.join(p, 'llmcore') for p in sys.path if os.path.isdir(os.path.join(p, 'llmcore', 'model_cards'))]
for b in [os.path.join(d, 'model_cards', 'default_cards', P) for d in dirs]:
    if not os.path.isdir(b): continue
    r = []
    for f in sorted(os.listdir(b)):
        if not f.endswith('.json'): continue
        try:
            d = json.load(open(os.path.join(b, f)))
            if d.get('model_type') != 'chat': continue
            if d.get('lifecycle', {}).get('status') in ('retired', 'deprecated'): continue
            ctx = d.get('context', {}).get('max_input_tokens', '?')
            pi = d.get('pricing', {}).get('per_million_tokens', {}).get('input', '?')
            r.append({'value': d['model_id'], 'label': d.get('display_name', d['model_id']), 'description': f'{ctx} ctx, ${pi}/M in'})
        except Exception: pass
    print(json.dumps(r))
    sys.exit()
print('[]')
PYEOF
discover.description: Load available huggingface chat models from llmcore model cards

fields.title: Timeout
fields.description: Request timeout in seconds.  Some HF-hosted models have cold-start latency; increase this if you see timeouts.

fields.title: Routing Provider
fields.description: Backend provider for request routing.  By default, requests go to HF's own Inference API.  Set to route through a third-party provider (billed to your HF account).

fields.title: Bill To (Organization)
fields.description: HuggingFace organization namespace to bill Inference API usage to.  Leave empty for personal billing.

sections.title: Storage: Session & Vector
sections.description: Persistence backends for conversation history and RAG documents.

fields.title: Type
fields.description: Type: Determines the storage backend. - "json": Simple, human-readable. Good for low-concurrency apps. - "sqlite": (Default) Excellent for single-process apps and local dev. - "postgres": Recommended for production, especially with multiple workers.
fields.recommendation: SQLite for single-process/dev. PostgreSQL for production with multiple workers.

fields.title: Path
fields.description: Path: Filesystem path for "json" and "sqlite" backends. The `~` character is expanded to your home directory.

fields.title: Db Url
fields.description: Database URL: Full connection URL for "postgres". It is STRONGLY recommended to set this via an environment variable.

fields.title: Type
fields.description: Type: Determines the vector database backend. - "chromadb": (Default) Easy to set up for local development. - "pgvector": Excellent for production if you already use PostgreSQL. - "pgvector_legacy": Uses original PgVectorStorage
fields.recommendation: ChromaDB for local dev. pgvector if you already run PostgreSQL in production.

fields.title: Default Collection
fields.description: Default Collection: A namespace for RAG documents. Allows you to maintain multiple distinct knowledge bases in the same vector store.

fields.title: Path
fields.description: Path: Filesystem path for file-based vector stores like "chromadb".

fields.title: Db Url
fields.description: Database URL: Connection URL for "pgvector". It is STRONGLY recommended to set this via an environment variable.

sections.title: Storage: Observability
sections.description: Instrumentation, metrics, event logging, and tracing for storage operations.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Master switch for all observability features. When false, all observability components are disabled with zero overhead.

fields.title: Log Queries
fields.description: Log ALL queries at DEBUG level. WARNING: Very verbose. Use only for debugging specific issues.

warning_if.message: Very verbose. Use only for debugging specific issues.

fields.title: Log Slow Queries
fields.description: Log queries that exceed the slow query threshold at WARNING level. Recommended to keep enabled for production monitoring.

fields.title: Slow Query Threshold Seconds
fields.description: Threshold (in seconds) for slow query detection. Queries taking longer than this will be logged and recorded.

fields.title: Include Query Params
fields.description: Include query parameters in logs. WARNING: Security risk - may expose sensitive data in logs.

warning_if.message: Security risk — may expose sensitive data in logs.

fields.title: Metrics Enabled
fields.description: Enable metrics collection (counters, histograms, gauges).

fields.title: Metrics Backend
fields.description: Metrics backend type. - "prometheus": Expose metrics via HTTP endpoint for Prometheus scraping - "memory": In-memory storage for testing/debugging - "none": Disable metrics collection

fields.title: Metrics Prefix
fields.description: Prefix for all metric names.

fields.title: Metrics Port
fields.description: Port for Prometheus metrics HTTP endpoint (when metrics_backend = "prometheus").

fields.title: Event Logging Enabled
fields.description: Enable persistent event logging to database. Events are stored in the storage_events table for audit and analysis.

fields.title: Event Retention Days
fields.description: Days to retain events in the database. Set to 0 to retain events forever (not recommended for production).

fields.title: Event Table Name
fields.description: Database table name for event storage.

fields.title: Event Batch Size
fields.description: Number of events to batch before flushing to database. Higher values reduce database writes but increase memory usage.

fields.title: Event Flush Interval Seconds
fields.description: Interval (in seconds) for flushing events to database. Events are flushed when batch_size is reached OR this interval elapses.

fields.title: Tracing Enabled
fields.description: Enable distributed tracing. Requires OpenTelemetry SDK to be installed and configured.

fields.title: Tracing Backend
fields.description: Tracing backend type.

fields.title: Tracing Endpoint
fields.description: OTLP collector endpoint for trace export.

fields.title: Tracing Service Name
fields.description: Service name for traces.

fields.title: Tracing Sample Rate
fields.description: Sampling rate for traces (0.0 to 1.0). 1.0 = trace all requests, 0.1 = trace 10% of requests.

sections.title: Model Card Library
sections.description: Metadata for LLM models — context limits, capabilities, pricing, lifecycle.

fields.title: User Cards Path
fields.description: Path to user-defined model cards directory. Cards in this directory override built-in cards with the same model_id. Organize cards by provider subdirectory: model_cards/<provider>/<model>.json The `~` character is expanded to your home directory.

fields.title: Auto Load
fields.description: Automatically load model cards on first registry access. Set to false if you want to control loading timing manually via registry.load()

fields.title: Strict Validation
fields.description: Validation strictness for model card files. If true, invalid JSON files will raise errors during loading. If false, invalid files are logged as warnings and skipped.

sections.title: Embedding Providers
sections.description: Provider-specific settings for embedding generation.

fields.title: Device
fields.description: Device for local Sentence Transformer models. Auto-detected if not set.

fields.title: Default Model
fields.description: API Key for OpenAI embeddings. api_key = ""

discover.command:
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json, os, sys
P = 'openai'
dirs = [os.path.join('src', 'llmcore')]
dirs += [os.path.join(p, 'llmcore') for p in sys.path if os.path.isdir(os.path.join(p, 'llmcore', 'model_cards'))]
for b in [os.path.join(d, 'model_cards', 'default_cards', P) for d in dirs]:
    if not os.path.isdir(b): continue
    r = []
    for f in sorted(os.listdir(b)):
        if not f.endswith('.json'): continue
        try:
            d = json.load(open(os.path.join(b, f)))
            if d.get('model_type') != 'embedding': continue
            if d.get('lifecycle', {}).get('status') in ('retired', 'deprecated'): continue
            ctx = d.get('context', {}).get('max_input_tokens', '?')
            pi = d.get('pricing', {}).get('per_million_tokens', {}).get('input', '?')
            r.append({'value': d['model_id'], 'label': d.get('display_name', d['model_id']), 'description': f'{ctx} ctx, ${pi}/M in'})
        except Exception: pass
    print(json.dumps(r))
    sys.exit()
print('[]')
PYEOF
discover.description: Load available openai embedding models from llmcore model cards

fields.title: Default Model
fields.description: API Key for Google AI (Gemini) embeddings. api_key = "" Default embedding model. As of 2026: - "gemini-embedding-001"      — GA, text-only, 3072 dims, 100+ languages - "gemini-embedding-2-preview" — multimodal (text+image+audio+video), 3072 dims DEPRECATED (shutdown Jan 2026): "text-embedding-004", "models/embedding-001"

discover.command:
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json, os, sys
P = 'google'
dirs = [os.path.join('src', 'llmcore')]
dirs += [os.path.join(p, 'llmcore') for p in sys.path if os.path.isdir(os.path.join(p, 'llmcore', 'model_cards'))]
for b in [os.path.join(d, 'model_cards', 'default_cards', P) for d in dirs]:
    if not os.path.isdir(b): continue
    r = []
    for f in sorted(os.listdir(b)):
        if not f.endswith('.json'): continue
        try:
            d = json.load(open(os.path.join(b, f)))
            if d.get('model_type') != 'embedding': continue
            if d.get('lifecycle', {}).get('status') in ('retired', 'deprecated'): continue
            ctx = d.get('context', {}).get('max_input_tokens', '?')
            pi = d.get('pricing', {}).get('per_million_tokens', {}).get('input', '?')
            r.append({'value': d['model_id'], 'label': d.get('display_name', d['model_id']), 'description': f'{ctx} ctx, ${pi}/M in'})
        except Exception: pass
    print(json.dumps(r))
    sys.exit()
print('[]')
PYEOF
discover.description: Load available google embedding models from llmcore model cards

fields.title: Default Model
fields.description: Host URL for the Ollama server for embeddings. host = "http://localhost:11434" Default Ollama model for embeddings. Ensure it's pulled locally.

discover.description: Fetch locally pulled Ollama models (shared cache)

transform.jmespath: models[].{value: name, label: name, description: details.family}

sections.title: Embedding Cache
sections.description: Two-tier caching (LRU memory + SQLite disk) for embedding vectors.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Master switch for embedding caching. Reduce API costs by caching identical embeddings.
fields.recommendation: Keep enabled to avoid redundant embedding API calls and reduce costs.

fields.title: Memory Size
fields.description: Maximum number of embeddings to keep in memory (LRU cache). Fast access to frequently used embeddings.

fields.title: Disk Enabled
fields.description: Enable SQLite disk persistence for embeddings. Cache survives process restarts.

fields.title: Disk Path
fields.description: Path to SQLite database for cached embeddings. Persistent storage location.

fields.title: Disk Max Entries
fields.description: Maximum number of embeddings to store on disk (0 = unlimited). Limit disk space usage.

fields.title: Ttl Hours
fields.description: Hours before cached embeddings expire (0 = never expire). Ensure cache freshness when embedding models change.

sections.title: Context Management & RAG
sections.description: Prompt assembly, truncation strategy, RAG retrieval, and templates.

fields.title: Inclusion Priority
fields.description: A comma-separated list defining the order in which context components are added to the prompt. Gives fine-grained control over the final prompt's structure. Valid Components: "system_history", "explicitly_staged", "user_items_active", "history_chat", "final_user_query". Default: "system_history,explicitly_staged,user_items_active,history_chat,final_user_query"

fields.title: Truncation Priority
fields.description: A comma-separated list defining the order in which context types are removed to meet token limits. Controls what information is sacrificed when the context is too long. Valid Components: "history_chat", "user_items_active", "rag_in_query", "explicitly_staged". Default: "history_chat,user_items_active,rag_in_query,explicitly_staged"

fields.title: Default Prompt Template
fields.description: The template used to format the final query when RAG is enabled. It will be formatted with `{context}` (retrieved documents) and `{question}` (the user's query). To instruct the LLM on how to use the provided RAG context.
fields.default:
You are an AI assistant specialized in answering questions about codebases
  based on provided context.
  Use ONLY the following pieces of retrieved context to answer the user's question.
  If the answer is not found in the context, state that you cannot answer based on the provided information.
  Do not make up an answer or use external knowledge. Keep the answer concise and relevant to the question.
  Include relevant source file paths and line numbers if possible, based *only* on the provided context metadata.

  Context:
  ---------------------
  {context}
  ---------------------

  Question: {question}

  Answer:

fields.title: Prompt Template Path
fields.description: Optional path to a custom prompt template file. Overrides the string above.

fields.title: Rag Retrieval K
fields.description: Default number of documents to retrieve for RAG.

fields.title: Reserved Response Tokens
fields.description: Number of tokens to leave free in the context window for the model's response.

fields.title: Minimum History Messages
fields.description: Minimum number of recent chat messages to try to keep during truncation.

fields.title: User Retained Messages Count
fields.description: Prioritizes keeping the N most recent user messages (and their preceding assistant responses) during truncation.

fields.title: Max Chars Per User Item
fields.description: A safeguard character limit for a single user-provided context item. The default is very large to accommodate large documents.

sections.title: Observability & Cost Tracking
sections.description: API usage tracking, cost estimation, and latency metrics.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Top-level observability enable (affects all observability features)

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Enable recording of API usage and cost estimation. Budget monitoring and usage analytics.

fields.title: Db Path
fields.description: Path to SQLite database for cost records. Persistent storage for usage analytics.

fields.title: Retention Days
fields.description: Days to retain cost records (0 = forever). Manage database size.

fields.title: Log To Console
fields.description: Log usage records to console. Real-time visibility during development.

fields.title: Track Latency
fields.description: Record request latency with usage records. Performance monitoring.

sections.title: Autonomous: Core & Goals
sections.description: Autonomous operation — goal management and decomposition.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Master switch — set to false to disable all autonomous capabilities

fields.title: Persist Goals
fields.description: Enable persistent goal storage across sessions

fields.title: Storage Path
fields.description: Path to goals storage file (JSON format). Supports ~ expansion and ${ENV_VAR} substitution.

fields.title: Auto Decompose
fields.description: Automatically decompose high-level goals into sub-goals via LLM

fields.title: Max Sub Goals
fields.description: Maximum number of sub-goals per parent goal (1–50)

fields.title: Max Goal Depth
fields.description: Maximum nesting depth for goal hierarchies (1–10)

fields.title: Max Attempts Per Goal
fields.description: Maximum retry attempts before marking a goal as failed (1–100)

fields.title: Base Cooldown Seconds
fields.description: Base cooldown between retry attempts in seconds (exponential backoff)

fields.title: Max Cooldown Seconds
fields.description: Maximum cooldown cap in seconds for exponential backoff

sections.title: Autonomous: Heartbeat
sections.description: Periodic task scheduler for autonomous operation.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Enable the heartbeat task scheduler

fields.title: Base Interval
fields.description: Base heartbeat interval in seconds (1–3600)

fields.title: Max Concurrent Tasks
fields.description: Maximum number of heartbeat tasks running concurrently (1–20)

sections.title: Autonomous: Escalation
sections.description: Human escalation framework — webhook and file notification channels.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Enable human escalation

fields.title: Auto Resolve Below
fields.description: Auto-resolve escalations below this level without human intervention. Levels (ascending severity): debug, info, advisory, action, urgent, critical

fields.title: Dedup Window
fields.description: Deduplication window in seconds — identical escalations within this window are suppressed (0 = no deduplication, max 86400)

sections.title: Autonomous: Resource Monitoring
sections.description: Hardware limits, API cost limits, and token budgets.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Enable resource monitoring

fields.title: Check Interval
fields.description: Resource check interval in seconds (5–600)

fields.title: Max Cpu Percent
fields.description: Hardware limits

warning_if.message: Temperature above 85°C may cause hardware damage. Critical for SBCs (e.g. Raspberry Pi).

fields.title: Max Hourly Cost Usd
fields.description: API cost limits (USD)

fields.title: Max Daily Cost Usd
fields.recommendation: Set conservatively at first; monitor actual usage via cost tracking before increasing.

fields.title: Max Hourly Tokens
fields.description: Token limits

sections.title: Autonomous: Context Synthesis
sections.description: Adaptive context window management for autonomous sessions.

fields.title: Max Context Tokens
fields.description: Maximum tokens for the synthesized context window

fields.title: Compression Threshold
fields.description: Compression triggers when context usage exceeds this fraction (0.0–1.0)

fields.title: Prioritization Strategy
fields.description: Context prioritization strategy: recency_relevance — balance recency and semantic relevance (default) relevance_only    — rank purely by semantic similarity recency_only      — rank purely by temporal proximity

sections.title: Agents: Core
sections.description: Agent execution limits and sandbox mode selection.

fields.title: Max Iterations
fields.description: Default maximum iterations for agent loops

fields.title: Default Timeout
fields.description: Default timeout for agent tasks (in seconds)

fields.title: Mode
fields.description: Sandbox mode determines which backend to use for isolation. - docker: Use Docker containers (recommended for most use cases) - vm: Use SSH to a dedicated VM (for high-security scenarios) - hybrid: Try Docker first, fall back to VM if Docker unavailable

fields.title: Fallback Enabled
fields.description: Enable fallback to VM if primary mode fails (only applies to hybrid mode)

sections.title: Agents: Sandbox Docker
sections.description: Docker container settings for isolated agent code execution.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Enable Docker sandbox support

fields.title: Image
fields.description: Default Docker image for sandbox containers. Should be a Python image with necessary tools.

discover.description: List locally available Docker images suitable for sandbox

fields.title: Image Whitelist
fields.description: Whitelist of allowed Docker images (glob patterns supported). Only images matching these patterns can be used.

fields.title: Full Access Label
fields.description: Docker label that grants FULL access level. Containers with this label bypass tool restrictions.

fields.title: Full Access Name Patterns
fields.description: Container name patterns that grant FULL access (glob patterns).

fields.title: Memory Limit
fields.description: Memory limit for containers (Docker memory limit format).
fields.recommendation: 1g is safe for most Python tasks. Increase for data processing or ML workloads.

fields.title: Cpu Limit
fields.description: CPU limit for containers (number of CPU cores).

fields.title: Timeout Seconds
fields.description: Default timeout for operations in seconds.

fields.title: Network Enabled
fields.description: Enable network access in containers. WARNING: Enabling network access reduces isolation security.

warning_if.message: Enabling network access reduces sandbox isolation security.

sections.title: Agents: Sandbox VM
sections.description: SSH-based VM sandbox for high-security agent execution.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Enable VM sandbox support (requires SSH access to a VM).

fields.title: Host
fields.description: VM host address.

fields.title: Port
fields.description: SSH port.

fields.title: Username
fields.description: SSH username.

fields.title: Private Key Path
fields.description: Path to SSH private key file. WARNING: Use SSH agent or environment variable in production.

fields.title: Use Ssh Agent
fields.description: Use SSH agent for authentication instead of key file.

fields.title: Full Access Hosts
fields.description: List of hosts that grant FULL access level. VMs in this list bypass tool restrictions.

fields.title: Timeout Seconds
fields.description: Default timeout for operations in seconds.

fields.title: Working Directory
fields.description: Working directory on the VM.

sections.title: Agents: Sandbox Volumes & Tools
sections.description: Volume mounts, tool access control, and output tracking.

fields.title: Share Path
fields.description: Host path for shared data that persists across sandbox instances. This directory is mounted read-write in sandboxes.

fields.title: Outputs Path
fields.description: Host path for agent output files. Files saved here persist after sandbox cleanup.

fields.title: Allowed
fields.description: List of tools allowed for RESTRICTED access level sandboxes. FULL access sandboxes bypass this restriction.

fields.title: Denied
fields.description: List of tools explicitly denied for all access levels. These tools are never available, even for FULL access.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Enable persistent tracking of agent outputs.

fields.title: Max Log Entries
fields.description: Maximum number of execution log entries per run.

fields.title: Max Run Age Days
fields.description: Maximum age of runs to keep (in days). 0 = keep forever.

fields.title: Max Runs
fields.description: Maximum number of runs to keep. 0 = no limit.

sections.title: Agents: Circuit Breaker
sections.description: Detect and interrupt failing agent loops.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Enable circuit breaker protection
fields.recommendation: Keep enabled in all environments to prevent runaway costs.

fields.title: Max Iterations
fields.description: Maximum iterations (overrides agent max_iterations as hard limit)

fields.title: Max Same Errors
fields.description: Trip after N identical consecutive errors

fields.title: Max Execution Time Seconds
fields.description: Trip after N seconds of total execution time

fields.title: Max Total Cost
fields.description: Trip if total cost exceeds this amount (USD)

fields.title: Progress Stall Threshold
fields.description: Trip if progress stalls for N iterations (no output change)

fields.title: Progress Stall Tolerance
fields.description: Progress similarity tolerance (0.0 = exact match, 1.0 = any difference)

sections.title: Agents: Activity System
sections.description: XML-based structured output for models without native tool/function calling.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Enable activity system as fallback for models without native tool support

fields.title: Fallback To Native Tools
fields.description: Fall back to native tools if activity parsing fails

fields.title: Max Per Iteration
fields.description: Maximum activities per LLM output iteration

fields.title: Max Total
fields.description: Maximum total activities per session

fields.title: Default Timeout Seconds
fields.description: Default timeout for activity execution (seconds)

fields.title: Total Timeout Seconds
fields.description: Total timeout for activity loop (seconds)

fields.title: Stop On Error
fields.description: Stop processing activities on first error

fields.title: Parallel Execution
fields.description: Execute activities in parallel when possible

fields.title: Max Observation Length
fields.description: Maximum observation length in characters

sections.title: Agents: Capability Check
sections.description: Pre-flight capability validation before agent execution.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Enable pre-flight capability checking

fields.title: Use Model Cards
fields.description: Consult model card registry for capability information

fields.title: Use Runtime Query
fields.description: Use runtime query to verify capabilities (if model card not found)

fields.title: Strict Mode
fields.description: Strict mode: fail if model doesn't support required capabilities When false, falls back to activity system for non-tool models

fields.title: Suggest Alternatives
fields.description: Suggest alternative models if capability check fails

sections.title: Agents: Human-in-the-Loop
sections.description: Human approval workflows for sensitive agent operations.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Enable HITL approval system
fields.recommendation: Keep enabled for any agent that can execute code or make external requests.

fields.title: Global Risk Threshold
fields.description: Global risk threshold for requiring approval

fields.title: Default Timeout Seconds
fields.description: Default timeout for approval requests (seconds)

fields.title: Timeout Policy
fields.description: Policy when approval times out

warning_if.message: Auto-approve on timeout may allow dangerous actions to proceed without human review.

fields.title: Batch Similar Requests
fields.description: Batch similar approval requests

fields.title: Audit Logging Enabled
fields.description: Enable audit logging of all approval decisions

fields.title: Audit Log Path
fields.description: Path for HITL audit log (JSONL format)

sections.title: Agents: Model Routing
sections.description: Intelligent routing based on task complexity, cost, and capability.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Enable model routing

fields.title: Strategy
fields.description: Routing optimization strategy

fields.title: Fallback Enabled
fields.description: Enable fallback to alternative models on failure

sections.title: Agents: Goal Classification
sections.description: Complexity classification for fast-path routing and iteration limits.

fields.title: Classifier Enabled
fields.description: Enable goal complexity classification When enabled, goals are analyzed before execution to determine complexity.

fields.title: Use Llm Fallback
fields.description: Use LLM for uncertain classifications Falls back to LLM when heuristic confidence is below threshold. Slower but more accurate for edge cases.

fields.title: Heuristic Confidence Threshold
fields.description: Confidence threshold for heuristic classification If heuristic confidence is below this, uses LLM fallback (if enabled).

fields.title: Trivial Max Iterations
fields.description: Maximum iterations by complexity level These override max_iterations based on goal complexity.

sections.title: Agents: Fast Path
sections.description: Fast-path executor for trivial goals — bypasses full cognitive cycle.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Enable fast-path for trivial goals When enabled, trivial goals (like "hello") skip the full cognitive cycle.

fields.title: Cache Enabled
fields.description: Use cached responses for identical/similar queries

fields.title: Cache Max Entries
fields.description: Maximum cache entries

fields.title: Cache Ttl Seconds
fields.description: Cache TTL in seconds (1 hour default)

fields.title: Templates Enabled
fields.description: Use template responses for known trivial patterns

fields.title: Max Response Time Ms
fields.description: Maximum response time before timeout (milliseconds)

fields.title: Temperature
fields.description: Temperature for fast-path LLM calls

fields.title: Max Tokens
fields.description: Maximum tokens for fast-path responses

fields.title: Fallback On Timeout
fields.description: Fall back to full cognitive cycle on timeout

sections.title: Agents: Darwin Enhancements
sections.description: Failure learning, TDD support, and multi-attempt arbiter.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Enable Darwin agent enhancements When false, Darwin uses basic cognitive cycle without enhancements.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Enable failure learning system When true, failures are logged and retrieved for future reference. When false, failure learning is disabled (no-op).

fields.title: Backend
fields.description: Storage backend for failure logs - sqlite: File-based storage (recommended for development) - postgres: PostgreSQL database (recommended for production)

fields.title: Db Path
fields.description: SQLite database path (used when backend = "sqlite")

fields.title: Db Url
fields.description: PostgreSQL connection URL (used when backend = "postgres") postgresql://user:password@host:port/database

fields.title: Max Failures To Retrieve
fields.description: Maximum number of similar failures to retrieve before planning Higher values provide more context but increase prompt size.

fields.title: Min Pool Size
fields.description: Minimum number of connections in pool

fields.title: Max Pool Size
fields.description: Maximum number of connections in pool

fields.title: Table Prefix
fields.description: Table name prefix (useful for multi-tenant deployments)

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Enable TDD workflow When true, tests are generated before implementation code.

fields.title: Default Framework
fields.description: Default test framework

fields.title: Min Tests
fields.description: Minimum number of tests to generate

fields.title: Max Iterations
fields.description: Maximum TDD iteration cycles (generate tests → code → verify → retry)

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Enable multi-attempt generation When true, generates N candidates and uses arbiter to select best.

fields.title: Num Candidates
fields.description: Number of candidates to generate Higher values increase quality but also cost and latency.

fields.title: Temperatures
fields.description: Temperature values for candidate generation Different temperatures produce diverse solutions.

fields.title: Use Llm Arbiter
fields.description: Use LLM-based arbiter for selection When true, uses LLM to score and select best candidate. When false, uses simple heuristics.

sections.title: Agents: Observability
sections.description: Structured event logging, metrics, replay, sinks, and performance.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Master switch for the entire observability system. Quickly disable all observability features without changing other settings.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Enable/disable structured event logging to file. Capture detailed execution traces for debugging and analysis.

fields.title: Log Path
fields.description: Path to the JSONL file where events are written. Persistent storage for execution events. Events are written as newline-delimited JSON (JSONL/NDJSON).

fields.title: Min Severity
fields.description: Filter events by minimum severity level. Control verbosity of event logging.

fields.title: Categories
fields.description: Whitelist of event categories to capture. Selectively enable/disable logging for specific event types. "metric", "memory", "sandbox", "rag" Empty list means log ALL categories.

fields.title: Strategy
fields.description: Rotation strategy: "none", "daily", "size", "both" - none: No rotation, single file grows indefinitely - daily: Rotate at midnight each day - size: Rotate when file exceeds max_size_mb - both: Rotate on either condition

fields.title: Max Size Mb
fields.description: Maximum log file size in MB before rotation (if strategy includes "size").

fields.title: Max Files
fields.description: Number of rotated log files to keep. 0 = keep all.

fields.title: Compress
fields.description: Compress rotated files with gzip.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Enable write buffering for better performance. Batch multiple events before writing to reduce I/O overhead.

fields.title: Size
fields.description: Maximum number of events to buffer before flushing. Balance between I/O efficiency and data freshness.

fields.title: Flush Interval Seconds
fields.description: Maximum time (seconds) to hold events before flushing. Ensure events are persisted even during low activity.

fields.title: Flush On Shutdown
fields.description: Flush all buffered events when agent shuts down. Prevent data loss on graceful shutdown.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Enable/disable execution metrics collection. Track performance, cost, and usage statistics.

fields.title: Collect
fields.description: Whitelist of metric types to collect. Control which metrics are tracked. - "iterations": Cognitive loop iterations - "llm_calls": LLM API calls (latency, tokens, cost) - "activities": Tool/activity executions - "hitl": Human approval metrics - "errors": Error counts and types - "duration": Execution timing - "tokens": Token usage - "cost": Estimated costs Empty list means collect ALL metrics.

fields.title: Track Cost
fields.description: Enable estimated cost calculation for LLM calls. Budget monitoring and optimization.

fields.title: Track Tokens
fields.description: Track input/output token counts per call. Usage analysis and optimization.

fields.title: Latency Percentiles
fields.description: Percentiles to calculate for latency metrics. Understand latency distribution (p50, p95, p99).

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Enable execution replay functionality. Debug and analyze past executions step-by-step. Requires events.enabled = true

fields.title: Cache Enabled
fields.description: Cache parsed events in memory for faster replay. Speed up repeated replay operations.

fields.title: Cache Max Executions
fields.description: Maximum number of executions to cache. Limit memory usage.

fields.title: File Enabled
fields.description: Primary sink - writes to JSONL file (configured above in events.log_path)

fields.title: Memory Enabled
fields.description: Store events in memory for debugging/testing. Inspect events without file I/O during development. WARNING: Memory usage grows with event count. Use only for debugging.

fields.title: Memory Max Events
fields.description: Maximum events to keep in memory sink. 0 = unlimited.

fields.title: Callback Enabled
fields.description: Call a user-defined function for each event. Custom integrations (e.g., send to external monitoring). Callback must be registered programmatically, this just enables it.

fields.title: Async Logging
fields.description: Write events asynchronously to avoid blocking execution. Minimize observability overhead on agent performance.

fields.title: Sampling Rate
fields.description: Sample rate for high-frequency events (0.0 to 1.0). Reduce volume for very active agents. Lifecycle and error events are never sampled.

fields.title: Max Event Data Bytes
fields.description: Maximum size (bytes) for event data fields. Prevent huge events from bloating log files. Large data is truncated with "[TRUNCATED]" marker.

fields.title: Overhead Warning Threshold Percent
fields.description: Warn if logging overhead exceeds this percentage of execution time. Detect performance problems from observability.

sections.title: Semantiscan: Core & Database
sections.description: RAG/ingestion engine — database and vector backend configuration.

fields.title: Enabled
fields.description: Top-level semantiscan enablement flag (for future use)

fields.title: Path
fields.description: type = "chromadb"  # Currently only ChromaDB is supported by semantiscan Default path - should be synchronized with llmcore's vector storage path

sections.title: Semantiscan: Metadata Store
sections.description: External metadata store for ingestion history and Git tracking.

fields.title: Table Name
fields.description: Primary table for storing rich chunk metadata

fields.title: Ingestion Log Table Name
fields.description: Table for tracking ingestion state per repo/branch

fields.title: File History Table Name
fields.description: Table for tracking file changes per commit ('historical_delta' mode)

sections.title: Semantiscan: Embeddings
sections.description: Embedding model configurations for chunking ingestion.

sections.title: Semantiscan: Chunking
sections.description: File type parsing and chunking strategies for ingestion.

sections.title: Semantiscan: Ingestion
sections.description: Ingestion pipeline behaviour and Git-aware modes.

sections.title: Semantiscan: LLM
sections.description: LLM configuration for query answering and advanced features.

sections.title: Semantiscan: Retrieval
sections.description: RAG retrieval parameters — top_k, hybrid search, BM25.

sections.title: Semantiscan: File Discovery
sections.description: File inclusion/exclusion rules during ingestion.

sections.title: Semantiscan: Logging
sections.description: Semantiscan-specific logging (separate from unified logging).

post_write.title: Create data directories
post_write.description: Create the default directories used by llmcore for storage, logs, and caches.
